explanation: The attention mechanism computes a weighted sum of value vectors, where the weights are determined by the similarity between query and key vectors using scaled dot-product, followed by a softmax normalization.

The Transformer architecture processes all input tokens in parallel using self-attention layers. Positional encodings are added to token embeddings to inject information about the order of tokens in the sequence.

LLMs generate text autoregressively by predicting the next token given all previous tokens. Sampling strategies such as temperature scaling and top-p sampling control the randomness and diversity of the generated output.

RLHF fine-tunes a language model using human preference data. First, a reward model is trained on human comparisons of model outputs. Then, the LLM is optimized using reinforcement learning to maximize the reward model's score.

Word embeddings represent words as dense vectors in a high-dimensional space such that semantically similar words are close together. Transformer-based models produce contextual embeddings where the same word can have different representations depending on its surrounding context.
